title: Environmental health professionals
description: Review, evaluate, and analyze work environments and design programs and procedures to control, eliminate, and prevent disease or injury caused by chemical, physical, and biological agents or ergonomic factors. May conduct inspections and enforce adherence to laws and regulations governing the health and safety of individuals. May be employed in the public or private sector. Collect data on work environments for analysis by occupational health and safety specialists. Implement and conduct evaluation of programs designed to limit chemical, physical, biological, and ergonomic risks to workers. Provide and manage health education programs that help individuals, families, and their communities maximize and maintain healthy lifestyles. Use data to identify community needs prior to planning, implementing, monitoring, and evaluating programs designed to encourage healthy lifestyles, policies, and environments. May link health systems, health providers, insurers, and patients to address individual and population health needs. May serve as resource to assist individuals, other health professionals, or the community, and may administer fiscal resources for health education programs. (Based on 10 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Write reports and prepare or review specifications for safety equipment to ensure compliance with health and safety standards.
Conduct safety training or education programs and provide new-employee health and safety orientations.
Develop, conduct, or coordinate health needs assessments and public health surveys, including community health needs assessments.
Analyze incident data to identify trends in injuries, illnesses, accidents, or other hazards, and investigate accidents to determine causes.
Prepare and distribute health education materials to address smoking, vaccines, and other public health concerns.
Order suspension of activities that pose threats to workers' health or safety and recommend protective measures for hazardous work methods.
Inspect or evaluate workplace environments, equipment, and practices to ensure compliance with safety standards and regulations.
Investigate health-related complaints and inspect facilities to ensure compliance with public health legislation and regulations.
Develop or maintain hygiene programs, such as noise and ventilation surveys, and carry out medical monitoring programs for employees.
Collect samples of potentially toxic materials for analysis and investigate the adequacy of ventilation and other health-related workplace conditions.
Develop and present health education and promotion programs, including training workshops and community presentations.
Inspect work areas or operating equipment to ensure conformance to established health and safety standards.
Participate in continuing education to stay abreast of industry trends and developments relevant to environmental health.
Meet with managers or other supervisors to stay informed of changes affecting health and safety operations.
Recommend and implement measures to improve work methods, worker motivation, or community health programs.
Train workers in proper operational procedures to ensure compliance with health and safety regulations.
Confer with other personnel to exchange information or to resolve health and safety related problems.
Provide education to workers about specific health and safety needs of different groups within the community.
Resolve complaints regarding health and safety services rendered or issues with worker performance.
Direct or coordinate the activities of health and safety workers involved in inspections and program implementation.
Inform and explain company policies related to health and safety to new and experienced workers.
